_note: Disaster-recovery scenarios for the FRAMEWORK ITSELF (agent memory, andon pipeline, repo hygiene). recovery_script paths resolve to real engine scripts. DRILLED 2026-05-31: the three automated scenarios were run and their recovery times measured (last_measured_rta); the manual repo-history scenario has a documented procedure and a realistic manual RTO (not auto-measurable).

scenarios.title: Agent memory graph corruption or accidental loss
scenarios.impact: Self-improvement loops lose accumulated lessons/skills; retros no longer feed the engines.
scenarios.last_measured_rta: 0.07s (drill 2026-05-31) — restores from snapshots far under the 15m RTO

scenarios.title: Pipeline stuck in andon-halt state
scenarios.impact: No new waves can dispatch until the halt is cleared.
scenarios.rpo_target: 0 (halt is pre-merge; no committed work lost)

scenarios.title: Capture a memory snapshot before a risky migration
scenarios.impact: Preventive: ensures DR-MEMORY-01 has a recent restore point.

scenarios.title: Secret or personal data leaked into git history before publish
scenarios.impact: Privacy/security breach on publish to a public remote.
scenarios.rto_target: 30m manual (git filter-repo to purge + force-push + rotate the exposed secret)
scenarios.last_measured_rta: manual — not auto-measurable; pre-publish-guard prevents this reaching a remote, so the drill is the prevention gate, not a timed restore
